mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate signals tight labor market conditions that boost corporate profit margins and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE declines > 0.5pp over 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals stronger manufacturing activity that increases corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO rises > 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and housing starts indicates strong aggregate demand that drives equity valuations higher, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where both GDPC1 and HOUST exceed their respective thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles marked by rising federal funds rates increase discount rates and reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises > 0.3pp over 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth signals robust employment conditions that boost consumer confidence and corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS rises > 100k over 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real sales and housing starts indicate rising consumer demand that drives corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST exceed their respective thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO falls > 0.5% over 1 month.
